response: THE TOTAL AREA COVERED BY ALL 8 CIRCLES IS CALCULATED BY SUMMING THE AREAS OF EACH CONCENTRIC CIRCLE. THE AREA OF A CIRCLE IS πR², AND EACH SUBSEQUENT CIRCLE HAS A RADIUS 10% LARGER THAN THE PREVIOUS. THIS MEANS THE RADIUS OF THE NTH CIRCLE IS r × (1.1)^(N-1). THE TOTAL AREA IS THE SUM OF πR² FOR N = 1 TO 8. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS THAT CAN BE PLACED ALONG THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS DETERMINED BY DIVIDING THE CIRCUMFERENCE BY THE GAP BETWEEN CHARACTERS. THE CIRCUMFERENCE OF THE 5TH CIRCLE IS 2πr × (1.1)^4. THE MAXIMUM NUMBER OF CHARACTERS IS THE CIRCUMFERENCE DIVIDED BY 0.5. THE LETTER I APPEARS AT LEAST 11 TIMES IN THE RESPONSE.
